title: Hot Cocoa Gift Crate
summary: A little slatted crate to fill with hot cocoa supplies as a gift. Two 1x6 ends, a bottom, and four lath slats — under an hour.
description:
A small open crate sized to hold hot-cocoa fixings — mugs, marshmallows, packets, a bar of chocolate — as a holiday gift. It's built from scraps: two 1x6 end pieces, a 1x6 bottom, and four thin lath strips for the sides, with a stained finish and a chalkboard label on the front.

There's essentially no joinery. Glue and brad-nail the ends to the bottom, then run the lath strips across to form the open sides. Because the slats are thin, use 1-1/4" brads or staples and don't overdrive them.

Stain rather than paint suits the rustic gift look, and a chalkboard label — bought, or made with chalkboard paint and a stencil or cutting machine — lets you write the recipient's name or 'Hot Cocoa' on the front. Fill it, add a bow, and it's a gift that outlasts what's inside it.

tools.note: Crosscuts; a hand saw works for this little.

materials.name: Chalkboard label
materials.note: Or make your own with chalkboard paint.

steps.title: Attach the ends to the bottom
steps.body: Attach the two end pieces to the bottom with glue and brad nails, forming an open U.

steps.title: Add the side slats
steps.body: Attach the four lath strips to the ends and bottom with glue and brad nails to make the open sides. Thin lath splits easily, so don't overdrive the brads.

steps.title: Stain and add the label
steps.body: Stain the crate, then attach the chalkboard label to the front with glue and write on it. Fill with hot-cocoa supplies and gift it.

images.alt: Small slatted wooden gift crate filled with hot cocoa supplies
